title: Writing - Part 1: Public Transport
description: Your class has been discussing how to improve public transport in cities and reduce private car use.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the solutions in your notes. You should explain which solution you think is more effective, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Lower fares would encourage more people to use trains and buses instead of driving.
Dedicated cycle lanes and bike-sharing schemes make cycling a safe and practical choice.
Congestion charges and pedestrian zones can significantly reduce traffic in busy urban areas.

structure.introduction: Introduce the problems caused by car dependence in cities and the need for alternatives.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first solution and how it could reduce car use.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second solution and argue which is more effective.
structure.conclusion: Give your final recommendation for improving urban mobility.

content.modelAnswer:
Traffic congestion and air pollution are pressing problems in cities around the world. Two solutions that could encourage people to leave their cars at home are reducing public transport fares and restricting cars in city centres.

Making public transport cheaper is a direct and immediate incentive for commuters. When buses and trains are affordable, people are less likely to justify the cost of owning and running a car. Many cities that have experimented with free or heavily subsidised fares have seen significant increases in ridership and corresponding falls in traffic.

Restricting cars in city centres through congestion charges or pedestrian zones can also be highly effective. By making driving inconvenient or costly, authorities push people towards cleaner alternatives. Barcelona and Oslo, for example, have successfully reduced urban traffic with these policies.

In conclusion, both measures are effective, but I believe reducing fares offers a more inclusive solution. Not everyone can afford to pay congestion charges, so cheaper public transport ensures that sustainable travel is accessible to all residents.
